children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary awards grants to four State educational agencies, in accordance with paragraph (2), to pay the administrative expenses incurred in participating in the demonstration program under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary awards Federal Pell Grants to participating students in accordance with this section and consistent with .section 1070a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: From amounts appropriated under subsection (h) for a fiscal year, the Secretary is authorized to award grants to four State educational agencies to enable the State educational agencies to pay the administrative expenses incurred in participating in the demonstration program under this section by carrying out a demonstration project under which eighth grade students described in subsection (b)(1)(B) receive a commitment early in the students’ academic careers to receive a Federal Pell Grant.

children.heading: Equal amounts
children.label: B Equal amounts
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants under this section in equal amounts to each of the four participating State educational agencies.

children.label: i
children.text: one cohort of eighth grade students who begin participating in the first academic year for which funds have been appropriated to carry out this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: one cohort of eighth grade students who begin participating in the academic year succeeding the academic year described in clause (i).

children.heading: Students in each cohort
children.label: B Students in each cohort
children.text: Each cohort of students shall consist of not more than 10,000 eighth grade students who qualify for a free or reduced price school lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) or the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 175142 U.S.C. 1771

children.heading: Student data
children.label: 2 Student data
children.text: The State educational agency shall ensure that student data from local educational agencies serving students who participate in the demonstration project, as well as student data from local educational agencies serving a comparable group of students who do not participate in the demonstration project, are available for evaluation of the demonstration project, and are made available in accordance with the requirements of (commonly known as the “Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974”).section 1232g of this title

children.heading: Federal Pell Grant commitment
children.label: 3 Federal Pell Grant commitment
children.text: Each student who participates in the demonstration project receives a commitment from the Secretary to receive a Federal Pell Grant during the first academic year that the student is in attendance at an institution of higher education as an undergraduate, provided that the student applies for Federal financial aid (via the FAFSA or EZ FAFSA) for such academic year.

children.heading: Application process
children.label: 4 Application process
children.text: Each State educational agency shall establish an application process to select local educational agencies within the State to participate in the demonstration project in accordance with subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: Local educational agency participation
children.label: 5 Local educational agency participation
children.text: Subject to the 10,000 statewide student limitation described in paragraph (1), a local educational agency serving students, not less than 50 percent of whom are eligible for a free or reduced price school lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) or the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 ( et seq.), shall be eligible to participate in the demonstration project.42 U.S.C. 175142 U.S.C. 1771

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State educational agency desiring to participate in the demonstration program under this section shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the proposed targeted information campaign for the demonstration project and a copy of the plan described in subsection (f)(2);

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the student population that will receive an early commitment to receive a Federal Pell Grant under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: an assurance that the State educational agency will fully cooperate with the ongoing evaluation of the demonstration project; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: the number and quality of State educational agency applications received;

children.label: i
children.text: financial responsibility;

children.label: ii
children.text: administrative capability;

children.label: iii
children.text: commitment to focusing resources, in addition to any resources provided on students who receive assistance under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.];20 U.S.C. 6311

children.label: iv
children.text: ability and plans to run an effective and thorough targeted information campaign for students served by local educational agencies eligible to participate in the demonstration project; and

children.label: v
children.text: ability to ensure the participation in the demonstration project of a diverse group of students, including with respect to ethnicity and gender.

children.label: A
children.text: the number and quality of local educational agency applications received;

children.label: i
children.text: financial responsibility;

children.label: ii
children.text: administrative capability;

children.label: iii
children.text: commitment to focusing resources on students who receive assistance under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965;

children.label: iv
children.text: ability and plans to run an effective and thorough targeted information campaign for students served by the local educational agency; and

children.label: v
children.text: ability to ensure the participation in the demonstration project of a diverse group of students.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From amounts appropriated under subsection (h) for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall reserve not more than $1,000,000 to award a grant or contract to an organization outside the Department for an independent evaluation of the impact of the demonstration program assisted under this section.

children.heading: Competitive basis
children.label: 2 Competitive basis
children.text: The grant or contract shall be awarded on a competitive basis.

children.label: A
children.text: determine the number of students who were encouraged by the demonstration program to pursue higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: identify the barriers to the effectiveness of the demonstration program;

children.label: C
children.text: assess the cost-effectiveness of the demonstration program in improving access to higher education;

children.label: D
children.text: identify the reasons why participants in the demonstration program either received or did not receive a Federal Pell Grant;

children.label: i
children.text: were more likely to take a college-preparatory curriculum while in secondary school;

children.label: ii
children.text: submitted any applications to institutions of higher education; and

children.label: iii
children.text: took the PSAT, SAT, or ACT;

children.label: F
children.text: identify the number of students participating in the demonstration program who pursued an associate’s degree or a bachelor’s degree, or other postsecondary education;

children.label: G
children.text: compare the findings of the demonstration program with respect to participants to comparison groups (of similar size and demographics) that did not participate in the demonstration program; and

children.label: H
children.text: identify the impact of the demonstration program on the parents of students eligible to participate in the program.

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: 4 Dissemination
children.text: The findings of the evaluation shall be reported to the Secretary, who shall widely disseminate the findings to the public.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State educational agency receiving a grant under this section shall, in cooperation with the participating local educational agencies within the State and the Secretary, develop a targeted information campaign for the demonstration project assisted under this section.

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: A Outreach
children.text: A description of the outreach to students and the students’ families at the beginning and end of each academic year of the demonstration project, at a minimum.

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: B Distribution
children.text: A description of how the State educational agency plans to provide the outreach described in subparagraph (A) and to provide the information described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: aa
children.text: two-year public degree-granting institutions of higher education;

children.label: bb
children.text: four-year public degree-granting institutions of higher education; and

children.label: cc
children.text: four-year private degree-granting institutions of higher education;

children.label: aa
children.text: tuition and fees; and

children.label: bb
children.text: room and board;

children.label: I
children.text: the Federal Pell Grant amount, determined under , for which a student may be eligible for each award year;section 1070a(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: when and how to apply for a Federal Pell Grant; and

children.label: III
children.text: what the application process for a Federal Pell Grant requires;

children.label: iii
children.text: State-specific postsecondary education savings programs;

children.label: iv
children.text: State merit-based financial aid;

children.label: v
children.text: State need-based financial aid; and

children.label: vi
children.text: Federal financial aid available to students, including eligibility criteria for such aid and an explanation of the Federal financial aid programs under subchapter IV, such as the Student Guide published by the Department (or any successor to such document).

children.heading: Cohorts
children.label: 3 Cohorts
children.text: The information described in paragraph (2)(C) shall be provided annually to the two successive cohorts of students described in subsection (b)(1)(A) for the duration of the students’ participation in the demonstration project.

children.heading: Reservation
children.label: 4 Reservation
children.text: Each State educational agency receiving a grant under this section shall reserve not more than 15 percent of the grant funds received each fiscal year to carry out the targeted information campaign described in this subsection.

children.heading: Supplement, not supplant
children.label: g Supplement, not supplant
children.text: A State educational agency shall use grant funds received under this section only to supplement the funds that would, in the absence of such grant funds, be made available from non-Federal sources for students participating in the demonstration project under this section, and not to supplant such funds.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 2009 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years.
